instructions.instruction: Use `rules` instead of deprecated `only`/`except` for job conditions
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `only: [master]` or `only: [main]`... `only`/`except` are deprecated in GitLab 15+; `rules` is the current approach... BAD: `only: - main`... GOOD: `rules: - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'`
instructions.relevant_when: Any job that should conditionally run based on branch, tag, or pipeline type

instructions.instruction: Pin Docker images to specific versions — never use :latest
instructions.original_snippets: Pin Docker images to specific versions (never `:latest`)... BAD: `image: node:latest`... GOOD: `image: node:20.11-alpine3.19`
instructions.relevant_when: Any job or default block that specifies a Docker image

instructions.instruction: Use `needs` keyword for DAG optimization to allow jobs to run as soon as their direct dependencies complete rather than waiting for the whole stage
instructions.original_snippets: `needs` keyword for DAG optimization... Without needs: runs sequentially (slow)... With needs: runs in parallel (fast)
instructions.relevant_when: Pipelines with multiple jobs in the same or different stages that have specific inter-job dependencies

instructions.instruction: Always set `expire_in` on artifacts — never omit it
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER omit `expire_in` on artifacts... Artifacts without an expiration date are retained indefinitely... BAD: `artifacts: paths: [dist/]` with no `expire_in`... GOOD: `artifacts: paths: [dist/] expire_in: 7 days`
instructions.relevant_when: Any job that produces artifacts

instructions.instruction: Never hardcode secrets or credentials in .gitlab-ci.yml — use CI/CD variables
instructions.original_snippets: Masked variables for secrets; never hardcode credentials... BAD: `deploy --token abc123xyz`... GOOD: `deploy --token $DEPLOY_TOKEN`
instructions.relevant_when: Any job that connects to external services, APIs, registries, or deployment targets

instructions.instruction: Declare explicit `environment` block (name and url) for all deployment jobs
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER deploy to production without an environment and approval gate... A deployment job with no `environment:` key... GOOD: `environment: name: production url: https://example.com`
instructions.relevant_when: Jobs that deploy to any environment (staging, production, review apps)

instructions.instruction: Use `resource_group` to prevent concurrent deployments to the same environment
instructions.original_snippets: `resource_group` for deployment jobs... Missing Resource Groups for Deployments — concurrent deployments possible
instructions.relevant_when: Deployment jobs where parallel runs would cause race conditions

instructions.instruction: Set explicit `timeout` on ALL jobs (10–30 minutes typically)
instructions.original_snippets: **Explicit `timeout` on ALL jobs** (10–30 minutes typically)... Set appropriate timeouts to prevent jobs from hanging
instructions.relevant_when: Every job in any pipeline

instructions.instruction: Use `retry` for flaky operations such as network calls, external APIs, and deployments
instructions.original_snippets: `retry` for flaky operations (network, external APIs)... Configure retry for flaky operations to improve reliability
instructions.relevant_when: Jobs that perform network operations, external API calls, or deployments

instructions.instruction: Use `extends` (preferred) or YAML anchors for DRY configuration — avoid duplicate `before_script` blocks in every job
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER define duplicate `before_script` blocks in every job... GOOD: Define a `.node_setup` hidden job with `before_script: [npm ci]` and use `extends: .node_setup` in dependent jobs... Use `extends` for inheritance (preferred over YAML anchors)
instructions.relevant_when: Pipelines with multiple jobs sharing common setup (image, cache, before_script)

instructions.instruction: Do NOT hardcode runner tags for every job — add tags only to jobs that genuinely require specific runner capabilities
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER hardcode runner tags for every job... Hardcoding forces all jobs onto specific runners even when generic runners would work... BAD: `tags: [kubernetes, production]` on lint and unit test jobs.
instructions.relevant_when: Pipelines where some but not all jobs need specialized runners

instructions.instruction: Configure caching for dependencies (npm, pip, maven, etc.) with appropriate cache key and pull/pull-push policy
instructions.original_snippets: Caching for dependencies (npm, pip, maven, etc.)... Use appropriate cache keys (branch, commit, files)... Set `policy: pull` for jobs that only read cache... Set `policy: pull-push` for jobs that update cache
instructions.relevant_when: Pipelines that install dependencies before build/test steps

instructions.instruction: Use `when: manual` and require a manual approval gate for production deployment jobs
instructions.original_snippets: Require manual approval for production deployments... `when: manual` for production jobs... Protected environments for production
instructions.relevant_when: Any job deploying to a production environment

instructions.instruction: Use kebab-case for job and stage names; use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for variables; use lowercase for environment names
instructions.original_snippets: Semantic stage and job names (kebab-case)... Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for variables... Use lowercase for environment names
instructions.relevant_when: Writing any .gitlab-ci.yml configuration

instructions.instruction: Set `interruptible: true` on test/build jobs so a newer pipeline can cancel them
instructions.original_snippets: `interruptible: true` for test jobs... Mark test jobs as interruptible to save resources
instructions.relevant_when: Build and test jobs in any pipeline

instructions.instruction: Use specific artifact paths (not ./**) and exclude sensitive file patterns with the `exclude` key
instructions.original_snippets: Be explicit about artifact paths... Use `exclude` to prevent sensitive files... BAD: `artifacts: paths: [./**]`... GOOD: specific paths with `exclude: ["**/*.env", "**/*.pem"]`
instructions.relevant_when: Any job that produces artifacts

instructions.instruction: Use `$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA` for image tagging to ensure unique, traceable image versions
instructions.original_snippets: image tagging strategy (`$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA`)
instructions.relevant_when: Docker build pipelines that push images to a registry

instructions.instruction: Output a Reference Analysis confirmation block (Pipeline Pattern, Best Practices, Security Guidelines, Template Foundation) before generating the pipeline
instructions.original_snippets:
After reading references, output this confirmation before proceeding:
## Reference Analysis Complete
**Pipeline Pattern Identified:**...
**Best Practices to Apply:**...
**Security Guidelines:**...
**Template Foundation:**...
instructions.relevant_when: At the start of any complete pipeline generation task

instructions.instruction: Avoid `curl | bash` patterns in CI scripts; download and verify scripts before executing
instructions.original_snippets: Never pipe curl directly to bash... Validate downloaded scripts... DANGEROUS: `curl https://install.sh | bash`... SECURE: download, verify sha256, then run
instructions.relevant_when: Jobs that download and execute external installation scripts

instructions.instruction: Validate every complete pipeline using `devops-skills:gitlab-ci-validator` before presenting to the user
instructions.original_snippets: Every complete pipeline MUST be validated before presenting to the user... invoke `devops-skills:gitlab-ci-validator`... Fix CRITICAL/HIGH issues and re-validate until clear
instructions.relevant_when: After generating any complete .gitlab-ci.yml
